nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official public-archives entry supports Senegal's 2023 national AI strategy summary document. Treat as non-binding strategy context.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Rwanda National AI Policy
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of ICT and Innovation
nationalAIRegulations.summary: National AI policy supporting Vision 2050 and the National Strategy for Transformation. Rwanda hosted the Global AI Summit on Africa (2025) and signed the Africa Declaration on AI.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Ministry of ICT and Innovation, Rwanda — National AI Policy
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Reviewed against the cited official or issuer-controlled source. Treat as national strategy/policy context, not binding AI law.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Slovakia National AI Strategy / Action Plan
nationalAIRegulations.status: In implementation; 2025 Media Services Act amendments aligned with EU DSA/AI Act
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Investments, Regional Development and Informatisation
nationalAIRegulations.summary: National AI strategy integrated in Slovakia's broader digital transformation policy. In 2025 the Media Services Act was amended to align with the EU Digital Services Act and authorities were designated for the EU AI Act.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Government of Slovakia — Ministry of Investments, Regional Development and Informatisation
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official MIRRI page supports Slovakia's AI-policy activity and links strategy/action-plan materials. Keep media-services and EU AI Act implementation details as caveated context, not standalone binding AI law.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Sweden's AI Strategy
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Government Offices of Sweden
nationalAIRegulations.summary: National AI strategy and action plan focused on top-ten AI competitiveness, public-sector adoption, skills, research, security, and responsible use.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Government Offices of Sweden — Sweden's AI Strategy
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official Government Offices page confirms Sweden's AI Strategy was published on 2026-02-25 with an accompanying action plan.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Switzerland — AI Strategy / Federal Council guidelines on AI
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Federal Office of Communications (OFCOM); Federal Council
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Federal guidelines on AI focused on responsible innovation, human-centred AI, and Switzerland's participation in international AI governance.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Swiss Federal Office of Communications — AI guidelines
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official OFCOM page confirms the Federal Council's November 2020 AI guidelines and their non-binding role for the Confederation.

nationalAIRegulations.name: New Zealand Algorithm Charter for Aotearoa
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Department of Internal Affairs; Stats NZ
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Government-wide framework on transparent and accountable use of algorithms by the New Zealand public sector. New Zealand joined the Bletchley Declaration in 2024.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Stats NZ — Algorithm Charter for Aotearoa
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Reviewed against the cited official or issuer-controlled source. Treat as national strategy/policy context, not binding AI law.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Egypt National AI Strategy
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Communications and Information Technology
nationalAIRegulations.summary: National AI strategy across AI for government, AI for development, capacity building, and international activities. Second edition published in 2024.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Egypt National Council for Artificial Intelligence - strategy second edition
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official Egyptian AI portal PDF supports the second-edition strategy. Treat as non-binding strategy context.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Nigeria National AI Strategy
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Federal Ministry of Communications, Innovation & Digital Economy
nationalAIRegulations.summary: National AI strategy with priorities on responsible AI development, sectoral application, talent, infrastructure, and ethical and inclusive AI.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: NCAIR / NITDA - Nigeria National AI Strategy
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Government AI centre PDF supports Nigeria's 2024 National AI Strategy. Treat as strategy context, not binding regulation.

nationalAIRegulations.name: Mauritius National AI Strategy
nationalAIRegulations.regulatorOrBody: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation
nationalAIRegulations.summary: Pioneering African AI strategy across manufacturing, healthcare, fintech, education, agriculture, and public administration.
nationalAIRegulations.sourceName: Ministry of Information Technology, Communication and Innovation, Mauritius
nationalAIRegulations.verificationNotes: Official ministry-hosted PDF supports the 2018 Mauritius AI Strategy. A newer 2026 National AI Strategy / FAIR Guidelines launch exists and should be represented separately only after a dedicated source pass.

subnationalAIRules.name: Illinois Artificial Intelligence Video Interview Act (820 ILCS 42)
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: ILGA statute page confirms the Act title, effective date, disclosure, consent, sharing, deletion, and reporting duties.
subnationalAIRules.summary: Requires employers using AI to analyse candidate video interviews to disclose use, obtain consent, and limit data sharing.

subnationalAIRules.name: New York City Local Law 144 — Automated Employment Decision Tools
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: NYC DCWP page confirms Local Law 144, AEDT bias-audit, notice, and July 5, 2023 enforcement details.
subnationalAIRules.summary: Requires employers to conduct annual independent bias audits of automated employment decision tools and to disclose their use to candidates.
subnationalAIRules.sourceName: NYC Department of Consumer and Worker Protection — AEDT

subnationalAIRules.name: New York General Business Law §349-A — Surveillance Pricing disclosure
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: New York Senate statute page confirms the 2025-07-11 revision and personalized algorithmic pricing disclosure requirement.
subnationalAIRules.summary: Requires disclosure when prices shown to consumers are personalised using algorithms / AI. First state-level surveillance-pricing law in the U.S.
subnationalAIRules.sourceName: New York State Senate — General Business §349-A

subnationalAIRules.name: California 2025 AI legislative activity package
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: Official Governor source confirms SB 53 and related California AI legislative activity. This remains a representative package marker, not a single codified act, and is not counted as binding law.
subnationalAIRules.status: Representative package entry; not a single codified act
subnationalAIRules.summary: Represents California's 2025 state-level AI lawmaking activity. SB 53 is separately tracked as the binding frontier-AI transparency law; this package marker is not counted as binding law until the broader package boundaries are verified bill by bill.

subnationalAIRules.name: California SB 53 — Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: California Legislature page shows SB 53 as chaptered; Governor source confirms signing of the Transparency in Frontier Artificial Intelligence Act.
subnationalAIRules.summary: First U.S. frontier-AI transparency law. Requires large frontier developers to publish safety frameworks, report safety incidents, and submit to whistleblower protections.

subnationalAIRules.name: France - EU AI Act implementation activity
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: Official CNIL annual-report source supports French EU AI Act implementation activity and CNIL's AI-governance role. The record has been softened because this source does not establish a comprehensive national implementation act.
subnationalAIRules.status: Implementation activity; specific national act not tracked
subnationalAIRules.summary: French EU AI Act implementation activity, including CNIL's AI governance and supervisory work. This entry is not treated as a national AI Act or binding implementation law until a specific official act is added.

subnationalAIRules.name: Germany - draft EU AI Act implementation
subnationalAIRules.verificationNotes: Official Bundestag release supports a 2025 draft implementation bill and BNetzA's proposed central role. Keep as proposed/draft until the act is promulgated.
subnationalAIRules.status: Draft; BNetzA proposed as central market surveillance authority
subnationalAIRules.summary: German implementation of the EU AI Act. BNetzA (Federal Network Agency) proposed as central market surveillance authority; sectoral coordination with BSI and BaFin.
subnationalAIRules.sourceName: German Bundestag - EU AI Act implementation bill

governanceDomains.label: Frontier / GPAI
governanceDomains.description: General-purpose and frontier-model governance, including safety frameworks and GPAI obligations.

governanceDomains.label: Public sector
governanceDomains.description: Rules or guidance for government use, procurement, transparency, and public-service deployment.

governanceDomains.label: Employment / hiring
governanceDomains.description: Automated employment decision tools, hiring audits, and worker-facing AI disclosures.

governanceDomains.label: Biometric identification
governanceDomains.description: Remote biometric identification, biometric categorisation, and related high-risk uses.

governanceDomains.label: Synthetic media
governanceDomains.description: Deepfakes, generated content, provenance, watermarking, labelling, and notice-and-removal regimes.

governanceDomains.label: Healthcare
governanceDomains.description: AI systems deployed in medical, health, or care settings.

governanceDomains.label: Finance
governanceDomains.description: Financial-services AI governance, algorithmic risk, and consumer-facing financial decisions.

governanceDomains.label: Education / children
governanceDomains.description: AI uses affecting education, students, children, and youth.

governanceDomains.label: Defense / autonomous weapons
governanceDomains.description: Military AI, autonomous weapons, and defense-sector AI governance.

governanceDomains.label: Cybersecurity / critical infrastructure
governanceDomains.description: Security duties, critical infrastructure, resilience, and cyber-risk controls for AI systems.

governanceDomains.label: Compute / cloud / chips
governanceDomains.description: AI compute, hyperscale cloud, export controls, chips, and infrastructure bottlenecks.

governanceDomains.label: Standards / conformity
governanceDomains.description: AI standards, harmonized standards, conformity assessment, assurance, and certification context.

governanceDomains.label: Public procurement
governanceDomains.description: AI procurement, public purchasing rules, vendor assurance, and government acquisition controls.

governanceDomains.label: Enforcement / litigation
governanceDomains.description: Observed enforcement, litigation, complaints, regulator decisions, and implementation actions.

governanceObligations.summary: The EU AI Act creates risk-based duties, including high-risk AI risk management and GPAI risk-management obligations under phased application.
governanceObligations.caveat: EU applicability is not the same as separate national enactment; member-state implementation and enforcement arrangements vary.
governanceObligations.verificationNotes: EUR-Lex confirms Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 and phased risk-based obligations. This obligation row is a workbench index into the official regulation, not a substitute for the legal text.

governanceObligations.summary: The EU AI Act includes transparency duties for specified AI systems and additional information duties for general-purpose AI model providers.
governanceObligations.caveat: Specific transparency duties depend on role, system type, and phased application date.

governanceObligations.summary: High-risk AI systems must satisfy conformity and quality-management requirements before placement on the EU market.
governanceObligations.caveat: Actual route depends on product sector, harmonized standards, notified bodies, and delegated implementation.

governanceObligations.summary: The EU AI Act prohibits specified AI practices, including certain manipulative, social-scoring, and biometric uses.
governanceObligations.caveat: Read the official legal text for exceptions, law-enforcement carve-outs, and phased applicability.

governanceObligations.summary: The CoE AI Convention requires parties to maintain measures addressing AI lifecycle risks and impacts on human rights, democracy, and rule of law.
governanceObligations.caveat: As of the snapshot, the Convention was not yet in force; treaty duties apply only for parties once entry-into-force conditions are met.
governanceObligations.sourceName: Council of Europe - Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence

governanceObligations.summary: Korea's AI Basic Act creates a national framework for high-impact AI and governance duties before its 2026 application date.
governanceObligations.caveat: Detailed obligations depend on subordinate rules and implementation guidance.

governanceObligations.summary: China's generative-AI measures require providers of public-facing generative AI services to satisfy filing, security, and content-governance expectations.
governanceObligations.caveat: This row summarizes the governance hook already tracked in the dataset; consult CAC sources for provider-specific filing details.
governanceObligations.sourceName: CAC - Interim Measures for Generative AI Services

governanceObligations.summary: China's AI rule stack includes generated-content governance and labelling expectations for public generative-AI services.
governanceObligations.caveat: Specific labelling obligations also interact with later synthetic-content measures not expanded in this row.
governanceObligations.sourceName: CAC - Interim Measures for Generative AI Services

governanceObligations.summary: California SB 53 requires covered frontier developers to publish safety frameworks.
governanceObligations.caveat: Coverage depends on statutory thresholds and the developer's connection to California.

governanceObligations.summary: California SB 53 requires covered frontier developers to report specified safety incidents.
governanceObligations.caveat: This is not a federal U.S. frontier-model law; scope is state-level and threshold-dependent.

governanceObligations.summary: The Seoul Frontier AI Safety Commitments ask participating companies to publish safety frameworks, thresholds, and severe-risk management processes.
governanceObligations.caveat: Company commitments are voluntary and should not be read as state law or treaty obligations.

governanceObligations.summary: The NIST GenAI Profile gives voluntary guidance for identifying, measuring, managing, and governing generative-AI risks.
governanceObligations.caveat: NIST guidance is influential technical guidance, not binding law by itself.
governanceObligations.sourceName: NIST - AI RMF Generative AI Profile

governanceObligations.summary: ISO/IEC 42001 provides AI management-system requirements for organizational governance across the AI lifecycle.
governanceObligations.caveat: ISO standards are voluntary unless adopted into contracts, procurement, or law.

governanceObligations.summary: NYC Local Law 144 requires annual independent bias audits for covered automated employment decision tools.
governanceObligations.caveat: The law is employment-specific and does not govern frontier models generally.
governanceObligations.sourceName: NYC DCWP - Automated Employment Decision Tools

governanceObligations.summary: The TAKE IT DOWN Act creates notice-and-removal duties for covered platforms handling nonconsensual intimate imagery, including AI-generated digital forgeries.
governanceObligations.caveat: This is a synthetic-media/content law, not broad frontier-model governance.

governanceObligations.summary: Australia's proposed mandatory guardrails would turn selected high-risk AI safety expectations into mandatory duties.
governanceObligations.caveat: This remains a proposal in the snapshot and must not be treated as binding law.
governanceObligations.sourceName: Department of Industry, Science and Resources - Proposed mandatory guardrails

governanceObligations.summary: Brazil's PL 2338/2023 would create a risk-based AI framework with duties for developers and operators.
governanceObligations.caveat: The bill remains proposed until enactment is verified through official sources.

governanceObligations.summary: Türkiye's draft AI law would create risk-assessment and compliance expectations for high-risk AI systems.
governanceObligations.caveat: The draft remains under legislative consideration and should not be treated as in force.
governanceObligations.sourceName: Grand National Assembly of Türkiye - Draft AI Law

governanceObligations.summary: Mexico's proposed federal AI law is tracked as a proposal for AI governance duties, including transparency-oriented obligations.
governanceObligations.caveat: Status is proposed and source pages are session-sensitive; do not treat as enacted law.

governanceObligations.summary: OpenAI's EU-facing general-purpose AI activity creates a conditional EU AI Act exposure hook captured in the lab exposure dataset.
governanceObligations.caveat: This is an applicability hook, not a public enforcement finding against the lab.

governanceObligations.summary: U.S. advanced-chip export controls are tracked as an infrastructure constraint affecting Chinese frontier-AI compute capacity.
governanceObligations.caveat: This is infrastructure governance context, not an AI-specific national law exposure row.

implementationMilestones.label: EU AI Act entered into force
implementationMilestones.summary: Regulation (EU) 2024/1689 entered into force on 1 August 2024, with phased application after that date.

implementationMilestones.label: EU AI Act phased application
implementationMilestones.summary: The Act applies through phased deadlines; the next broad deadline tracked here is 2 August 2026.
implementationMilestones.verificationNotes: This milestone intentionally uses a broad next-deadline cue. Detailed article-by-article deadlines should be added as separate rows before legal reliance.

implementationMilestones.label: EU member-state competent-authority deadline
implementationMilestones.summary: The Commission describes 2 August 2025 as the deadline for Member States to designate and empower national competent authorities under the AI Act.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: European Commission - Governance and enforcement of the AI Act
implementationMilestones.verificationNotes: Official Commission page states that each Member State should have designated and empowered national competent authorities until 2 August 2025. This milestone tracks the EU-level deadline, not verified country-by-country designations.

implementationMilestones.label: GPAI Code of Practice published
implementationMilestones.summary: The General-Purpose AI Code of Practice is tracked as voluntary EU implementation guidance for providers of GPAI models under the AI Act.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: European Commission - General-Purpose AI Code of Practice
implementationMilestones.verificationNotes: Official Commission page states that the Code was published on 10 July 2025, is voluntary, and helps providers demonstrate compliance with AI Act GPAI obligations.

implementationMilestones.label: CoE AI Convention adopted; not yet in force in snapshot
implementationMilestones.summary: The Convention was adopted and opened for signature, but the snapshot notes that entry-into-force conditions had not yet been met.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: Council of Europe - Framework Convention on Artificial Intelligence

implementationMilestones.label: Korea AI Basic Act adopted; application begins in 2026
implementationMilestones.summary: Korea's AI Basic Act is tracked as adopted with application beginning in 2026.

implementationMilestones.label: China generative-AI measures in force
implementationMilestones.summary: China's Interim Measures for generative-AI services are tracked as in force for public-facing generative-AI services.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: CAC - Interim Measures for Generative AI Services

implementationMilestones.label: California SB 53 signed
implementationMilestones.summary: California SB 53 is tracked as a binding state-level frontier-AI transparency law after signing.

implementationMilestones.label: Brazil PL 2338/2023 under consideration
implementationMilestones.summary: Brazil's AI framework bill remains tracked as proposed until enactment is verified.

implementationMilestones.label: Türkiye draft AI law under consideration
implementationMilestones.summary: Türkiye's draft AI law remains tracked as proposed pending official enactment.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: Grand National Assembly of Türkiye - Draft AI Law

implementationMilestones.label: Mexico federal AI proposal tracked
implementationMilestones.summary: Mexico's federal AI proposal is retained as proposed and not counted as binding law.

implementationMilestones.label: Norway AI Act implementation consultation
implementationMilestones.summary: Norway is tracked as proposed/implementation planning for EEA alignment with the EU AI Act.

implementationMilestones.label: UK pro-innovation AI regulation response issued
implementationMilestones.summary: The UK cross-regulator principles approach is tracked as guidance and institutional implementation context, not as an omnibus AI statute.

implementationMilestones.label: UK Artificial Intelligence (Regulation) Bill in House of Lords
implementationMilestones.summary: Private member's bill tracked as proposed; it is not counted as enacted UK AI law.

implementationMilestones.label: Italy Law No. 132/2025 in force
implementationMilestones.summary: Italy's national artificial-intelligence law is tracked as in force and includes competent-authority designations for EU AI Act implementation.

implementationMilestones.label: Italy designates ACN and AGID roles
implementationMilestones.summary: The Italian law record tracks ACN and AGID as competent national authorities for AI-system monitoring and inspection.

implementationMilestones.label: Slovenia EU AI Act implementation law in force
implementationMilestones.summary: Slovenia's EU AI Act implementation law is tracked as in force from the official PISRS record.

implementationMilestones.label: Germany draft EU AI Act implementation bill
implementationMilestones.summary: German implementation activity is tracked as draft/proposed pending promulgation.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: German Bundestag - EU AI Act implementation bill

implementationMilestones.label: France EU AI Act implementation activity tracked
implementationMilestones.summary: French activity is tracked as implementation context and supervisory work; no comprehensive national implementation act is counted here.

implementationMilestones.label: Spain draft AI law approved as preliminary draft
implementationMilestones.summary: Spain's EU AI Act implementation draft is tracked as proposed and not as an enacted national AI law.
implementationMilestones.sourceName: La Moncloa - Council of Ministers reference

indicatorSources.name: Government AI Readiness Index 2025
indicatorSources.coverage: 195 governments in the attached 2025 data workbook.
indicatorSources.sourceName: Oxford Insights - Government AI Readiness Index 2025
indicatorSources.caveat: Readiness score is a comparative policy-capacity indicator, not a legal-status finding or evidence of binding AI duties.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Imported from the attached Oxford Insights 2025 XLSX; source URL points to the issuer page for the index and methodology.

indicatorSources.name: AI and Democratic Values Index 2026
indicatorSources.publisher: Center for AI and Digital Policy
indicatorSources.coverage: 90 countries in the attached CAIDP 2026 CSV export.
indicatorSources.sourceName: CAIDP - AI and Democratic Values Index 2026
indicatorSources.caveat: CAIDP scores are a civil-society democratic-values assessment and should not be read as official legal status or treaty participation.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Imported from the attached CAIDP 2026 CSV export; source URL points to the issuer report page.

indicatorSources.name: UNESCO AI Ethics Readiness Assessment Methodology country hub
indicatorSources.coverage: Countries listed by UNESCO as in preparation, in process, completed, or with a country profile on the Global Hub.
indicatorSources.sourceName: UNESCO - Global AI Ethics and Governance Observatory Global Hub
indicatorSources.caveat: RAM status indicates UNESCO assessment/profile activity; it is not a comparable numeric readiness score and not a national legal obligation.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Parsed from the official UNESCO Global Hub country table and profile cards; subnational entries are listed in the review note rather than mapped as country-level records.

indicatorSources.name: Stanford Global AI Vibrancy Tool public-data score
indicatorSources.coverage: 66 countries in the official Global AI Vibrancy Tool full_data_09.24.25.csv public dataset.
indicatorSources.sourceName: Stanford HAI - Global AI Vibrancy Tool
indicatorSources.caveat: The score is contextual AI ecosystem evidence from the public tool data; it is not a legal-status or compliance score. Public country data vintage is 2024 even though the 2026 AI Index references the tool.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Computed from the official Global AI Vibrancy Tool CSV, codebook, metric weights, and group weights exposed by the public tool bundle.

indicatorSources.name: IMF AI Preparedness Index
indicatorSources.coverage: Official IMF DataMapper AI Preparedness Index source family; country rows are not imported in this pass because the public page blocked automated data retrieval.
indicatorSources.caveat: AI preparedness is a macro-context indicator. It is not legal status, treaty participation, or evidence of binding AI duties.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official IMF DataMapper source identified; automated fetch returned an access-control page, so this source is tracked without country score rows until a durable data feed is verified.

indicatorSources.name: OECD AI Observatory Index 2026
indicatorSources.coverage: Official OECD iLibrary source family for AI Observatory index evidence; country rows are source-only until the report data is normalized.
indicatorSources.caveat: OECD index evidence is policy and ecosystem context. It does not establish legal effect or national-law coverage.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Tracked as a source family for future normalized rows; no country score rows are imported in this pass.

indicatorSources.name: OECD.AI policy observatory
indicatorSources.coverage: Official OECD.AI policy source family for national AI policy initiatives and observatory context.
indicatorSources.caveat: Policy-observatory entries are context and discovery leads; they are not treated as legal status unless the underlying official national source is separately verified.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Tracked as a source family for future source-delta monitoring and policy database reconciliation.

indicatorSources.name: Global Index on Responsible AI
indicatorSources.coverage: Issuer source family for responsible-AI governance indicators; country rows are not imported until methodology and data are normalized into this app schema.
indicatorSources.caveat: Responsible-AI index scores are civil-society context indicators and do not establish binding legal duties or official compliance status.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Tracked as a source family only. Import country rows after a transparent methodology and machine-readable data crosswalk are reviewed.

indicatorSources.name: Netherlands public algorithm register
indicatorSources.coverage: Issuer-controlled source for public-sector algorithm and AI transparency registry practice; a Netherlands context row is included.
indicatorSources.sourceName: Government of the Netherlands - Algorithm Register
indicatorSources.caveat: Public-sector register evidence shows transparency infrastructure, not a general national AI-law score or proof of private-sector obligations.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official register page states that Dutch government organizations publish information about algorithms they use, focusing on impactful algorithms including high-risk AI systems.

indicatorSources.name: International Network of AI Safety Institutes
indicatorSources.coverage: Official network source family for AI safety institute coordination and technical-governance cooperation.
indicatorSources.sourceName: NIST - International Network of AI Safety Institutes
indicatorSources.caveat: Safety-institute coordination is governance context. It is not a treaty, national law, or binding company obligation unless a separate legal source says so.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Tracked as source-only context for future safety-institute and technical-governance expansion.

indicatorSources.name: European Commission AI Factories
indicatorSources.coverage: Official EU source family for AI Factories, EuroHPC infrastructure, and EU compute-investment signals; an EU context row is included.
indicatorSources.caveat: AI Factories indicate infrastructure and compute-capacity context. They are not national AI laws, treaty participation, or binding obligations by themselves.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official European Commission AI Factories page describes EuroHPC-backed AI Factories, operational factories/antennas, and AI-optimised supercomputing investment context.

indicatorSources.name: U.S. National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource pilot
indicatorSources.coverage: Official NSF source family for U.S. shared AI research infrastructure and compute-resource access; a U.S. context row is included.
indicatorSources.sourceName: NSF - National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource Pilot
indicatorSources.caveat: NAIRR is research-infrastructure context. It does not establish private-sector AI duties or binding AI-law coverage.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official NSF page describes NAIRR as national infrastructure providing the research and education communities access to AI compute, software, data, models, educational resources, and expertise.

indicatorSources.name: UK Guidelines for AI procurement
indicatorSources.coverage: Official GOV.UK source family for public-sector AI procurement guidance; a UK context row is included.
indicatorSources.caveat: Procurement guidance is public-sector practice context. It should not be treated as a general binding AI statute or as private-sector legal coverage.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official GOV.UK guidance states it is aimed at central government departments considering AI technology and provides AI-specific procurement considerations.

indicatorSources.name: European AI Office governance and enforcement role
indicatorSources.coverage: Official EU source family for AI Act implementation, GPAI supervision, national competent authority coordination, and enforcement architecture.
indicatorSources.sourceName: European Commission - Governance and enforcement of the AI Act
indicatorSources.caveat: Regulator/institutional context. This row identifies governance architecture and does not by itself establish country-level compliance or enforcement outcomes.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official European Commission page states that the European AI Office and national market surveillance authorities implement, supervise, and enforce the AI Act, and that the AI Office supervises GPAI model obligations.

indicatorSources.name: EU General-Purpose AI Code of Practice
indicatorSources.coverage: Official EU source family for GPAI Code chapters, signatory list, and AI Act compliance-support materials.
indicatorSources.sourceName: European Commission - General-Purpose AI Code of Practice
indicatorSources.caveat: The Code is described by the Commission as a voluntary compliance-support tool. It should not be read as a standalone treaty, national law, or proof that every listed company has identical legal obligations.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official European Commission page states that the GPAI Code of Practice was published on 2025-07-10, is voluntary, and helps providers demonstrate compliance with AI Act GPAI obligations.

indicatorSources.name: FTC Operation AI Comply enforcement sweep
indicatorSources.coverage: Official U.S. enforcement source family for deceptive or unfair AI claims and AI-enabled consumer-protection cases.
indicatorSources.caveat: Enforcement activity under general consumer-protection law. This is AI-relevant enforcement context, not a comprehensive AI statute or a country-level AI readiness score.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official FTC release describes five law-enforcement actions involving AI hype, deceptive AI claims, or AI-enabled unfair/deceptive conduct.

indicatorSources.name: OMB M-24-18 AI acquisition memorandum
indicatorSources.publisher: U.S. Office of Management and Budget
indicatorSources.coverage: Official U.S. federal source family for acquisition of artificial-intelligence systems and services by agencies.
indicatorSources.caveat: Federal public-procurement guidance context. It does not establish general private-sector AI duties or a national AI law by itself.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official OMB memorandum source identified for future public-procurement normalization and agency AI acquisition workflows.

indicatorSources.name: EU database for high-risk AI systems
indicatorSources.coverage: Official EU AI Act source family for high-risk AI system registration and public database infrastructure.
indicatorSources.caveat: Registry infrastructure context. The row tracks the source family for future database-record imports; it is not itself a list of registered systems.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: EUR-Lex AI Act Article 71 provides for an EU database containing registered high-risk AI system information. No database rows are imported in this pass.

indicatorSources.name: UK AI Security Institute
indicatorSources.publisher: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
indicatorSources.coverage: Official UK source family for frontier-model testing, advanced-AI risk research, and AI security institute activity.
indicatorSources.caveat: Safety-institute evidence is technical-governance context. It is not a treaty, national law, or binding company obligation unless a separate legal source says so.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official AISI page describes the institute as a state-backed organisation conducting research, infrastructure building, model testing, and risk-mitigation work for advanced AI.

indicatorSources.name: Japan AI Safety Institute
indicatorSources.coverage: Official Japan AISI source family for AI safety evaluation methods, standards, guidance, and international coordination.
indicatorSources.caveat: Safety-institute evidence is technical-governance context. It does not establish binding legal duties or country-level compliance status by itself.
indicatorSources.verificationNotes: Official Japan AISI page describes work on AI safety evaluation methods, standards, guidance support, private-sector coordination, and international cooperation.

dimensions.Scope: AI hype, deceptive AI claims, and AI-enabled unfair or deceptive conduct
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: AI-relevant enforcement context under general consumer-protection law, not a standalone AI statute

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: FTC - Operation AI Comply
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official FTC release states that Operation AI Comply announced five law-enforcement actions involving AI hype, deceptive AI claims, or AI-enabled unfair/deceptive conduct.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.Coverage: Federal agency acquisition of AI systems and services
dimensions.Hook: Agency procurement, risk-management, collaboration, and market-competition practices
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Federal public-procurement context; not a general private-sector AI law

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: OMB - M-24-18 AI Acquisition Memorandum
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official OMB memorandum states requirements and guidance for covered agencies acquiring AI systems and services, and states it does not create rights or obligations for contractors or the public.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.Function: AI Act implementation, GPAI supervision, and national competent-authority coordination
dimensions.Institutional hook: European AI Office and national market-surveillance authorities
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Institutional governance context; not a country-by-country enforcement outcome

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: European Commission - Governance and enforcement of the AI Act
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official Commission page states that the European AI Office and national market surveillance authorities implement, supervise, and enforce the AI Act.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.AI Act hook: Article 71 database for high-risk AI systems
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Registry infrastructure context; not a list of registered systems in this dataset

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: EUR-Lex - Regulation (EU) 2024/1689, Article 71
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: EUR-Lex AI Act Article 71 provides for an EU database containing registered high-risk AI system information.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.Infrastructure: AI Factories and EuroHPC AI-optimised supercomputing support
dimensions.Reported status: Commission page describes 19 AI Factories and 13 Antennas as operational
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Compute-investment context only; not national AI law or treaty participation

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: European Commission - AI Factories
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official Commission AI Factories page describes EuroHPC-backed AI Factories, operational factories/antennas, and AI-optimised supercomputing investment context.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.Access model: Research and education community access to compute, data, models, tools, and expertise
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Research-infrastructure context; not private-sector AI duties or binding AI-law coverage

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: NSF - National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official NSF page describes NAIRR as national infrastructure providing the research and education communities access to AI compute, software, data, models, educational resources, and expertise.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.Function: Advanced-AI risk research, model testing, infrastructure building, and mitigation work
dimensions.Institutional home: UK Department for Science, Innovation and Technology
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Technical-governance context; not treaty participation or binding company obligation

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: UK AI Security Institute
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official AISI page describes a state-backed organisation conducting research, building infrastructure, testing risk mitigations, and working with AI developers and governments.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.Function: AI safety evaluation methods, standards, guideline support, private-sector coordination, and international cooperation
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Technical-governance context; not binding legal duties by itself

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: Japan AI Safety Institute
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official Japan AISI page describes work on AI safety evaluation methods, standards, guideline support, private-sector support, and international coordination.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.Registry type: Public-sector algorithm and AI transparency register
dimensions.Reported status: Public register lists government-published algorithm descriptions
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Transparency infrastructure context; not a general national AI-law score

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: Government of the Netherlands - Algorithm Register
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official register page states that Dutch government organizations publish information about algorithms they use, focusing on impactful algorithms including high-risk AI systems.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

dimensions.Coverage: Central government departments and public-sector AI procurement practice
dimensions.Legal-status caveat: Guidance/procurement context; not a general private-sector AI statute

countryIndicatorScores.sourceName: GOV.UK - Guidelines for AI procurement
countryIndicatorScores.verificationNotes: Official GOV.UK guidance states it is aimed at central government departments considering AI technology and provides AI-specific procurement considerations.
countryIndicatorScores.notes: Context indicator only; does not affect binding-law map coloring or legal summaries.

infrastructureNodes.name: Advanced AI chips
infrastructureNodes.verificationNotes: NVIDIA primary source verifies H100 as a representative advanced AI GPU; broader chip ecosystem and bottleneck assessment are analytical synthesis.
infrastructureNodes.description: Leading-edge GPUs and AI accelerators (NVIDIA H100/B200, AMD MI300, Google TPU, custom ASICs from AWS, Meta, Microsoft, Apple). The single hardest bottleneck for frontier model training and inference. Concentrated supply means whoever controls fabrication, exports, and resale routes shapes who can build frontier systems at scale.
infrastructureNodes.scopeCaveat: Advanced AI chips are not themselves AI law — they are the material substrate that frontier AI governance constrains via export controls, procurement, and cloud allocation.

infrastructureNodes.name: Hyperscale cloud
infrastructureNodes.verificationNotes: Cloud-provider market structure is sourced from a secondary market-analysis provider and should be periodically refreshed.
infrastructureNodes.description: Large cloud providers — AWS, Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud, Alibaba Cloud, Tencent Cloud — that host frontier model training and inference. Their allocation decisions, security baselines, and content moderation policies act as a de-facto governance layer. Provider-side controls (model gating, API rate-limits, jurisdictional routing) operate independently of any treaty.
infrastructureNodes.scopeCaveat: Cloud providers are not regulators; they are private actors whose decisions function as governance because they hold scarce capacity.

infrastructureNodes.name: U.S. BIS export controls on advanced computing ICs
infrastructureNodes.verificationNotes: BIS source verifies the 2025 AI-chip export-control action; this record remains out-of-scope as AI-specific law and is included as frontier-AI infrastructure governance.
infrastructureNodes.description: Bureau of Industry and Security (BIS) export controls on advanced computing integrated circuits, gradually expanded since 2022 with country-by-country quota and licensing rules. Among the strongest geopolitical levers over frontier AI capacity — operates upstream of any AI-specific national law.
infrastructureNodes.scopeCaveat: Not an AI-specific instrument under the strict scope rule. Included here because doc1 (the seed brief) is explicit that this is one of the highest-leverage frontier-AI governance levers, and the assignment is about frontier-AI governance specifically.
infrastructureNodes.sourceName: U.S. BIS — Press release on AI chip controls

dependencyEdges.description: The United States uses BIS export controls as a central geopolitical lever over advanced AI hardware.
The EU implements the AI Act through the AI Office and national supervisory authorities.
China's CAC-led system coordinates the main rules for public generative AI deployment and content labelling.
The UK launched the frontier-focused summit process at Bletchley Park.
South Korea co-hosted the Seoul Summit and advanced the safety-science agenda.
France hosted the Paris AI Action Summit and convened the Statement on Inclusive and Sustainable AI.
Japan's AI governance documents visibly draw from OECD-type principles.
Korea's governance approach sits within the OECD-style trustworthy-AI ecosystem.
Singapore's AI governance framework is built to interoperate with global trustworthy-AI principles.
Australia's AI safety guidance is aligned with OECD-style human-centred AI principles.
Canada's voluntary and standards-oriented AI governance fits within OECD-style norms.
The G20 AI Principles largely internationalised the OECD approach.
The Hiroshima process builds on the broader trustworthy-AI norm ecosystem shaped by the OECD.

outOfScopeItems.name: EU General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: GDPR is a general data-protection regulation, not an AI-specific instrument. Its provisions affect AI systems but the law itself is not about AI governance.

outOfScopeItems.name: India Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP)
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: DPDP is a general data-protection statute. It is not an AI-specific instrument and is excluded from the main map data.

outOfScopeItems.name: U.S. BIS export controls on advanced AI chips
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: Export controls are powerful geopolitical levers but are not AI-governance instruments in the sense used here. They regulate trade in chips, not the development or deployment of AI systems.

outOfScopeItems.name: U.S.–Japan–Netherlands semiconductor export controls
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: Trilateral arrangement focused on semiconductor manufacturing equipment, not AI governance.

outOfScopeItems.name: Generic national cybersecurity laws
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: General cybersecurity statutes affect AI systems but are not AI-specific governance instruments.

outOfScopeItems.name: Generic national digital strategies
outOfScopeItems.reasonExcluded: Broad digital strategies are excluded unless they include explicit AI governance provisions.

sourceNotes.note: This map tracks AI-specific governance instruments only. It excludes general privacy, export-control, chip, semiconductor, and cybersecurity instruments unless they are explicitly AI-specific.
The Council of Europe Framework Convention on AI is not yet in force as of 19 May 2026. Entry into force requires five ratifications, including at least three Council of Europe member states.

Coverage for UNESCO and UNGA instruments is represented via 'covered_by_membership' across UN member states. This does not imply per-country sign-on; it reflects the multilateral nature of those instruments.
ISO/IEC standards are voluntary international standards. There are no country signatures; participation is via the global standardization system.
Company commitments, GPAI Council outputs, and NIST technical guidance are shown as governance context. They do not create country-level signature, ratification, or binding-law rows in this dataset.
CEN-CENELEC AI Act standards work is standardization infrastructure. It should not be read as a separate national AI law or explicit state endorsement.
The Frontier Model Forum is non-state / industry governance, not an intergovernmental instrument. It is shown for context but has no country-level participation rows.
The Paris AI Action Summit Statement on Inclusive and Sustainable AI was notably not signed by the United States or the United Kingdom.
This dataset is a May 2026 snapshot. Treaty and signature status can change quickly. Always verify time-sensitive details against official sources before relying on them.
